cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to answer the question. The passage talks about a driver, Mr. Williams, and his experience at a crossroads. Let's break down the key points: 1. At some crossroads, drivers have to stop when they come to the main road. 2. At other crossroads, drivers have to go slow, but they don't have to stop unless something is coming from the main road. 3. At still others, drivers don't have to stop or go slow because they are on the main road. Now, for Mr. Williams' specific situation: - He was driving home from work and came to a crossroad with a "SLOW" sign. - He slowed down when he came to the main road. - He looked both ways to check for oncoming traffic. - He then drove across without stopping completely. - He heard a police whistle and pulled over to the side of the road. - The policeman accused him of not stopping at the crossing. - Mr. Williams explained that the sign only said "SLOW" and he had indeed slowed down. - The policeman then realized that he was in the wrong street. The question is asking what Mr. Williams was doing when he was stopped by the policeman. The choices are: (A) was driving at a high speed (B) was driving onto the main road (C) was going to stop his car (D) was driving slowly Based on the passage, Mr. Williams saw a "SLOW" sign, so he slowed down.
